## Engram

### AI Agent Architect

**Employment type:** Contract
**Dates:** May 2026 - Present · 2 mos
**Location:** Albuquerque, New Mexico, United States · Remote
**LinkedIn helped me get this job**

```
Selected as AI Architect for Engram — a US AI-media agency rebuilding itself around AI agents that run a client's entire media department end-to-end.

How I was hired: founder Luka Madzarac asked Claude AI itself to recommend top AI engineers as the company entered Anthropic's Partner Network. My open-source portfolio came back as Claude's top recommendation. Luka confirmed publicly: "Claude Code surfaced Chan as one of the strongest matches."

What I work on:

• Designing Mission Control plus specialised agents that run a client's media operation — natural language in, finished campaigns out.
• MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration patterns — Anthropic's open standard for letting AI agents safely use real tools, APIs, and data.
• "API-as-UI" design — products where the AI's reasoning IS the interface, not a chat widget bolted onto a form.
• Anthropic Partner Network curriculum + Claude Certified Architect — Foundations (CCA-F) exam, on top of held credentials in AI Fluency and Claude Code in Action.
```

**Skills:** Model Context Protocol (MCP), Anthropic Claude, AI Agent Architecture, Multi-Agent Systems, AI System Design
**Media:** `Engram Media`

```
Programme: 12 weeks, April 25 – July 17, 2026. Curriculum thesis: "natural language is now the source code." Students don't write code by hand — they direct AI assistants (Cursor, Claude Code, Gemini CLI) to ship real applications.

• Week 8 outcome: each student owns a live AI-powered personal site — portfolio + AI-clone chatbot + blog + auto-generated CV — on Next.js + Neon Postgres + Drizzle + Vercel.
• Week 12 outcome: each team of 2–4 ships a deployed multi-user AI SaaS — habit coaches, AI story studios, dorm chore splitters. Public Demo Day with audience voting.
• Built the teaching platform myself at programming.chanmeng.org — Docusaurus on Cloudflare Pages, ~96% solo (211 of 220 non-merge commits). Hosts 5 cohorts side-by-side with a version-aware RAG assistant — students query their cohort's own materials and get answers from inside the course content.
• Fully bilingual EN / CN.
```

**Skills:** Curriculum Development, AI-Assisted Development, Technical Teaching, Docusaurus, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), Next.js

## Gavigo
_9 mos · Remote_
**Links:** <https://ire.gavigo.com/> · <https://gavigo.com/>

### Founding Engineer

**Dates:** Feb 2026 - Present · 5 mos
**Location:** 1007 N Orange St, 4th Floor, 4785, Wilmington, Delaware 19801, USA
**LinkedIn helped me get this job**

```
Promoted from Core Engineer to Founding Principal Engineer at Gavigo across three formalised contracts in 6 months. I own the Intelligence Layer of Gavigo IRE — Gavigo's "Instant Reality Exchange" platform, which lets users open straight into a game without an app-store install. Reports to founder & CEO Saba Gecgil.

What "instant" means in production:

• Users get into the experience in under 1 millisecond on average — roughly 5,000× faster than a normal app-store install funnel. Warm-pool hit rate 84.6%; 0 errors at 100 concurrent live users under load.
• 439 total platform commits, 426 (~97%) authored solo.

External validation (Feb 2026): Gavigo accepted into Google for Startups Cloud Program and NVIDIA Inception, with press coverage from feedvoice.com.

Hardest work shipped:

• Sub-millisecond restore via a 5-state engine with atomic Redis transitions and timestamped proof signals — every speed claim is auditable in production logs, not asserted.
• Cross-cloud migration: DigitalOcean (Singapore) → Google Cloud GKE (Belgium) in a single 30-minute zero-downtime cutover.
• 7-trigger AI rules engine deciding which games to pre-load, for whom, when.

Stack: Go, GKE Kubernetes, Redis, WebSocket, React Native + Expo, gpt-5.4-mini.
```

**Skills:** Go (Programming Language), Kubernetes, Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE), Redis, WebSocket, React Native, Distributed Systems
**Media:** `GAVIGO IRE - AI Container Orchestration Dashboard`

**Location:** Wilmington, Delaware, United States
**LinkedIn helped me get this job**

```
Original consulting engagement at Gavigo (signed October 17, 2025) to architect and ship the M1 prototype for Gavigo's Instant Reality Exchange (IRE) platform — the foundational system for eliminating friction between user intent and digital experience in the "post-app world." The prototype shipped here is what led Gavigo to amend my contract twice and promote me to Founding Principal Engineer with founder-level equity.

What I built:

• End-to-end M1 visualisation prototype demonstrating cross-domain content switching, AI-driven orchestration, and zero-latency "Instant Interaction Points" — the artefact Gavigo took into investor and partner conversations.
• Microservices architecture on Kubernetes + Docker, Next.js / React frontend with Python AI services — simulating intelligent workload management and proactive scaling.
• Rule-based AI orchestration engine — the early ancestor of today's production 7-trigger Rules Engine.
• Real-time WebSocket protocol with millisecond-precision timestamps so investors could see the "decide → patch → respond" chain happen live.
• Investor-facing dashboard exposing AI decision-making and resource allocation in real time.
```

**Skills:** Microservices Architecture, Kubernetes, Docker, Next.js, Python (Programming Language), WebSocket

**Dates:** Mar 2025 - Oct 2025 · 8 mos

```
Hired as Senior AI/ML Infrastructure Engineer in February 2025. The role covered Sanicle's intense active-build phase — 14 weeks of solo development that took the company from a legacy Bubble no-code site to a production B2B SaaS platform.

Two flagship products designed and shipped:

Engineering achievements that translated directly to business outcomes:
```

## CORDE
**Links:** <https://corde.nz/>

### Full Stack Developer

**Dates:** Jun 2024 - Nov 2024 · 6 mos
**Location:** Christchurch City, Canterbury, New Zealand · Hybrid

```
CORDE — a 30+-year-old, 164-staff Canterbury infrastructure and civil-construction company (CCNZ Canterbury Contractor of the Year; Construction Excellence National Award). Five months on-site at CORDE's Rolleston head office, reporting to Barry Clark (business sponsor) with Kullum Ladley as Workbench API specification author.

The business problem: CORDE's field crews work in rural Canterbury where mobile coverage drops out, but the company's central operations platform assumes connectivity. They needed a mobile app that could become the system of record for the moment of work and reconcile cleanly when connectivity returned.

What I built and shipped:

• Production-quality React Native offline-first mobile app — the field worker's tablet captures text, photos, and GPS coordinates simultaneously, stores them locally with integrity guarantees, and syncs back to Workbench on reconnection. Handles real conflict cases: duplicate IDs, partial sync, mid-batch connectivity drops.
• #1-most-prolific contributor — 227 of 539 commits (42%).
• Effectively sole author of the team documentation repository (140 of 143 commits, 97.9%).
• The app remains in active use today by CORDE's field crews — kept current with React Native upgrades and Android 16 KB page-size compliance.

Stack: React Native 0.83 + TypeScript + Native Base + SQLite + native geolocation/camera/maps.
```

**Skills:** React Native, TypeScript, Offline-First Architecture, Native Base, SQLite, Mobile Application Development
**Media:** `Weekly Mobile App Development Sprint Meetings` · `Weekly Mobile App Development Sprint Meetings` · `CORDE | Full Stack Developer`
